title: SecureDatagramChannel unprotect request_id lookup uses wrong dict
description:
File: python/src/lichen/coap/secure.py:409-410

In _send_protected, response handling uses .get() then checks membership separately:
```python
if msg.code.is_response() and msg.token in peer_ctx.pending_requests:
    request_id = peer_ctx.pending_requests.get(msg.token)
```

This is redundant - if the token is in the dict, .get() will return it. But more importantly, responses are unexpected in _send_protected() - this path handles outgoing messages we're sending, not responses.

The comment 'Determine request_id for responses' on line 407 suggests copy-paste from _unprotect. For outgoing messages, we should be storing request_id after protect(), not looking it up.

This indicates a logic error in the protect/unprotect flow.
